contents:
# Iranian lawmaker reveals China India Japan paying Hormuz fees despite US sanctions

## Overview
Iranian lawmaker Jafar Qadiri stated that China, India, Japan and others remain paying Iran transit fees for Strait of Hormuz passage. These payments continue despite US sanctions and warnings of penalties for such transactions. This demonstrates persistent demand for Iranian oil amid enforcement pressures.

## Details
Qadiri noted that Iranian oil exports reach global markets regardless of US restrictions and naval blockade. He highlighted that Washington has warned paying countries could face severe sanctions. The fees collection scheme relates to IRGC efforts to assert sovereignty over the Strait. IRGC commander warned of sustained retaliation to any short attacks. This follows US interdictions and IRGC vessel seizures. Axios reported CENTCOM preparing strike options.[3]

**Location**: Tehran, Iran

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @UANI, which covers United Against Nuclear Iran sanctions IRGC enforcement. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following United Against Nuclear Iran sanctions IRGC enforcement will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
